steps.title: Look for a broad mineral rule
steps.content: The student wants to make a generalization about minerals, so the answer should state a rule that applies beyond only calcite, apatite, and topaz. The notes give the general rule: minerals with larger Mohs scale numbers are harder than minerals with smaller numbers and can leave visible scratches on them.

steps.title: Reject specific examples
steps.content:
• Choice A - 'Based on their Mohs scale numbers, topaz (8) is harder than apatite (5), and apatite is harder than calcite (3).': This ranks only the three minerals named in the notes. It is accurate, but it is a specific comparison rather than a generalization about minerals.
  • Choice B - 'Topaz can leave visible scratches on calcite, which is why topaz has a higher number than calcite on the Mohs scale of mineral hardness.': This focuses only on topaz and calcite, so it does not turn the rule into a broader statement.

steps.title: Select the generalization
steps.content:
• Choice D - 'The Mohs scale can be used to order calcite, topaz, and apatite by their ability to scratch other minerals.': This still stays with the three listed minerals and describes what the scale can do with them.
  • Choice C - 'Any mineral with a Mohs number of 8, like topaz, can scratch a mineral with a Mohs number of 5, like apatite.': The phrase 'Any mineral' makes the claim general. Topaz and apatite serve as examples of the rule, not as the whole claim. The answer is C.
